topic: Prediction-batch review: PRED-105 (46° copper coil → +15% cucumber growth, closes 2026-04-18) — spot-check on whether pending verdict is appropriate given flagged arithmetic failure
change_summary: Decider queued for pre-closure spot-check (queue_id 149). Assessment integrated with our_verdict=pending plus flagged internal arithmetic inconsistency (sin θ = H₀/R_eq yields 25.25°, not 46°) and protocol gaps.
our_argument_summary: Assessed as pending pending window closure (2026-04-18). Flagged that the 46° angle cannot be derived from the prediction as written (sin θ = H₀/R_eq with H₀=8537, R_eq=20015 gives 25.25°; the numerically matching trig is 2·arctan(H₀/R_eq) = 46.20°), and that the protocol lacks sample size, randomization, blinding, and pre-registered statistical test. Kernel of truth acknowledged — bioelectromagnetics is a real field; ~46° N sits in the cucumber-growing belt.

per_prediction_verdicts.reasoning: Pending is the correct verdict at T-1 day from window close. Assigning a substantive verdict (refuted / unfalsifiable / confirmed) before the declared test window elapses would be premature and gives a dome defender an easy rebuttal ("you did not wait for the data"). The analyst has correctly pre-flagged the methodology gaps and derivation inconsistency so that the decider can apply a firm downgrade template immediately after 2026-04-18. The strongest alternative — "unfalsifiable-as-stated" now — is defensible but throws away information cheaply obtained by waiting one more day; default should be pending with a locked-in post-closure rubric.
per_prediction_verdicts.strongest_defender_counter: Transcription error charity: the prediction text cites sin θ = H₀/R_eq but 2·arctan(H₀/R_eq) = 46.20° numerically matches, so the author may have mislabelled the trig function. Do not tank a real biology result over a formula typo. Plus: the window has not closed; pending is the only legitimate verdict.
per_prediction_verdicts.our_response: The transcription-error reading is available to the dome author post hoc, but (a) as written the derivation is wrong, and (b) even using 2·arctan, combining H₀ (pole value) with R_eq (equatorial radius) is inconsistent with V50.6 H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619), which gives H(R_eq) ≈ 837 km, not 8,537 km. Any angle built from H₀ and R_eq as if H were constant is not a dome-geometric derivation under the model's own V50.6 formula — it is a 1D globe-like flat-disc trig identity. The post-closure rubric should therefore (i) downgrade to post-hoc/methodology failure on any PASS announcement lacking a pre-registered protocol, and (ii) log an expansion on the sin-vs-arctan retrofit as evidence for Part 2b (derivation-theatre pattern).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — PRED-105 is a biology experiment, no monitor.py automation. The only model-touching element is the asserted derivation sin θ = H₀/R_eq.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The claimed 46° is asserted without a live computation; the V50.6 raw-text gives H₀=8,537 km and semi-major axis a=20,015 km, with H(r)=8537·exp(-r/8619). With those parameters sin θ = H₀/R_eq yields 25.25° (not 46°), and 2·arctan(H₀/R_eq) yields 46.20° — but this identity uses H as if it were constant, contradicting V50.6 H(r).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Protocol is under-specified: no N, no blinding, no randomization, no pre-registered alpha, and "growth" is undefined (mass / length / leaf count / germination timing). The 15% target is numerical but the falsification criterion "no statistically significant difference" is operationally empty without a pre-committed N and α.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The analyst note correctly identifies the trig mismatch but does not explicitly call out the second, deeper V50.6 inconsistency: using H₀ (pole-height) with R_eq (equatorial radius) as if H were constant ignores the V50.6 H(r) exponential, which is the model's headline V12/V50.6 finding. This is worth flagging as an expansion hook if PRED-105 closes on a PASS.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: PRED-105 is a biology bench experiment; monitor.py contains no fetch logic for plant growth, cucumber, or coil-angle data. Automation is not applicable.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard-physics expectation of a +15% growth rate from a passive, unpowered copper coil wound at 46°. Bioelectromagnetics has small (<5%) documented effects for powered / pulsed EM exposures, but not for a passive coil.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-04-18. Registration predates any claimed result, so the prediction is prospective by timing. If the dome later publishes a matching outcome, assessment shifts to post-hoc only if the protocol was adjusted after observation — flag for reassessment.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The stated derivation (sin θ = H₀/R_eq → 46°) is arithmetically inconsistent with the V50.6 parameters; the numerically matching identity (2·arctan(H₀/R_eq) = 46.20°) is not the one cited and also mixes pole-height H₀ with equatorial radius in contradiction of V50.6 H(r)=8537·exp(-r/8619). Net: no coherent derivation from dome geometry.

holes_found.description: The assessment implicitly assumes 46° was meant to be derived from dome geometry and then retrofitted. A simpler reading is that 46° is the experimenter's latitude (46°N lies within cucumber-growing temperate belt and is a canonical bioelectromagnetics field-site latitude), with the sin θ = H₀/R_eq formula slapped on post hoc. Under this reading, the 2·arctan(H₀/R_eq) = 46.20° numerical coincidence is itself spurious and should not be offered as a charitable alternative — it is an after-the-fact retrofit finder for any angle near 46°. Not acknowledging this gives a dome defender a costume for the failure.
holes_found.recommendation: In post-closure expansion, note that 46° matches the geographic latitude band for both the experimenter and the model authors (probable), and that the sin-vs-2·arctan discrepancy is consistent with retrofit rather than typo. This strengthens the "derivation theatre" framing and ties into Part 2b (relabels-standard / derives-from-dome patterns).

holes_found.description: The assessment flags the wrong-trig issue but not the deeper V50.6 inconsistency: under H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619), at r = R_eq = 20,015 km the firmament height is ~837 km, not 8,537 km. Any formula that combines H₀ and R_eq as if H were constant is incompatible with the dome's own V50.6 headline result. This is a cleaner internal contradiction than the sin-vs-arctan mismatch because it holds regardless of which trig function is used.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence to the assessment detail_reasoning: "Moreover, V50.6 H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) yields H(R_eq) ≈ 837 km, so any expression of the form f(H₀, R_eq) treats H as constant, contradicting V50.6." Queue an expansion for Part 2b if PRED-105 resolves PASS — this becomes exhibit #N of the flat-H identity dressed up as dome geometry pattern.

holes_found.description: Testability_score is "low" but our_verdict remains "pending" rather than being pre-committed to a post-closure downgrade rubric. Window closes 2026-04-18 (tomorrow). Without a locked-in rubric, there is a risk that a retrospective PASS announcement gets accepted as a confirmation even though the protocol lacks N, blinding, randomization, and a pre-registered statistical test.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should, before window closure, add a note to the predictions.json entry specifying: (i) any PASS without a pre-registered protocol is downgraded to post-hoc/methodology failure; (ii) silent drop = logged in uncounted-failures.json; (iii) a null/negative result is recorded as falsified. This converts testability_score=low into a deterministic post-closure outcome and prevents the dome from getting an easy WIN on a loose protocol.

holes_found.description: The suggested_entry and the integrated predictions.json entry disagree on category: suggested_entry lists category="bioelectromagnetics"; predictions.json lists category="biology". Not a substantive issue, but worth harmonizing so downstream filters (e.g., dashboard facets) do not fragment.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider: pick one — recommend "bioelectromagnetics" since that is the mechanism space claimed; or add a secondary tag. Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: The 46° value collides with a real latitude band (temperate cucumber-growing region, ~40°–50°N) and is thus almost certainly latitude-derived, not geometry-derived. Arguing this directly is stronger than arguing about trig functions, because it explains WHY 46° (biology-realistic growing latitude) and also WHY the derivation is arithmetically broken (there is nothing to derive — the number was chosen first).
sources: FAO cucumber cultivation latitude bands (general horticulture references)

stronger_arguments.description: V50.6 H(r) exponential contradicts any H₀/R_eq identity. This is a stronger internal-consistency argument than the trig mismatch because it cannot be dismissed as a 'formula typo.' The dome's headline V12 insight is that H is NOT constant; treating it as constant in a featured prediction undermines the V12 claim.

Protocol vagueness itself is a falsification-theatre pattern. 'Controlled lab/home experiment with documented measurements' plus a 15% effect size without N/α is Popper-empty: the experimenter can always find a protocol variant that shows ≥15%, or declare any failure a protocol artifact. This is a cross-WIN consistency point — many dome predictions have the same shape (specific numerical target, vague measurement rubric).
sources: Part 2b uncounted-failures pattern; Part 5 kill-shots framework

kernel_of_truth.description: Bioelectromagnetics is a legitimate research domain; weak static and low-frequency EM fields have documented small effects on seed germination, enzyme kinetics, and callus growth in plants. Copper-wound Helmholtz-like coils are a recognisable laboratory form factor. 46° N is a realistic growing latitude for cucumbers. The claim therefore lives adjacent to a real literature rather than being pure fabrication.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Documented bioelectromagnetic effects on plants are typically <5%, require powered / pulsed exposures, tightly controlled growth chambers, and rarely survive independent replication. A passive unpowered coil produces no field to first order — there is no proposed mechanism that is both (a) angle-sensitive at plant length scales and (b) capable of a 15% growth delta. And regardless of biology plausibility, the dome's derivation of 46° from its own parameters fails under V50.6.

tldr_review.tldr_notes: The TLDR on predictions.json PRED-105 matches detail_reasoning; factual, does not overclaim, flags both biology and arithmetic issues. Minor cleanup: TLDR says 'yields 25°' where precise value is 25.25° but rounding is fine for a TLDR.

recommended_edits: Add V50.6 H(r) inconsistency to detail_reasoning (moderate hole #2).
Add explicit post-closure rubric to predictions.json notes field before 2026-04-18 (moderate hole #3).
Harmonize category field between suggested_entry and predictions.json (minor hole #4).
Keep our_verdict=pending; no change needed before window closes.
deeper_analysis: This spot-check was requested specifically because the decider wanted adversarial cover on leaving our_verdict=pending despite a flagged derivation failure. The answer is: yes, pending is correct at T-1 day — early verdicts hand the dome an easy 'you did not wait' rebuttal and contribute nothing we cannot do on 2026-04-18. The real work is making pending cost the dome something: a locked-in post-closure rubric so a silent PASS cannot convert to a WIN. The deeper interest is the pattern itself — 46° chosen first, formula retrofitted after, protocol vague enough to admit any outcome. That pattern matches the Part 2b taxonomy for derives_from_dome=false and relabels_standard=false, and should be cross-linked when the expansion is written.

citations_verified: H₀ = 8,537 km and semi-major axis a = 20,015 km — verified in raw-text/05-model.txt lines 28–34.
V50.6 H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) — verified in raw-text/05-model.txt lines 14–16, 33.
citations_unchecked: Specific bioelectromagnetics effect-size literature cited in kernel_of_truth — general claim widely supported but exact effect-size range not independently re-checked this run.

batch_summary.note: Single-prediction batch. Agree with analyst pending verdict with recommended minor_edit (add V50.6 H(r) inconsistency) and a pre-closure rubric commitment.
